info.title: SonarQube Web API
info.description: OpenAPI specification generated from SonarQube web services definition. See the official SonarQube API documentation for detailed response schemas.

tags.name: api/analysis_reports
tags.description: Get details about Compute Engine tasks.

tags.name: api/ce
tags.description: Get information on Compute Engine tasks.

tags.name: api/components
tags.description: Get information about a component (file, directory, project, ...) and its ancestors or descendants. Update a project or module key.

tags.name: api/duplications
tags.description: Get duplication information for a project.

tags.name: api/features
tags.description: Provides information about features available in SonarQube

tags.name: api/languages
tags.description: Get the list of programming languages supported in this instance.

tags.name: api/measures
tags.description: Get components or children with specified measures.

tags.name: api/navigation
tags.description: Get information required to build navigation UI components

tags.name: api/notifications
tags.description: Manage notifications of the authenticated user

tags.name: api/permissions
tags.description: Manage permission templates, and the granting and revoking of permissions at the global and project levels.

tags.name: api/plugins
tags.description: Manage the plugins on the server, including installing, uninstalling, and upgrading.

tags.name: api/project_badges
tags.description: Generate badges based on quality gates or measures

tags.name: api/qualitygates
tags.description: Manage quality gates, including conditions and project association.

tags.name: api/rules
tags.description: Get and update some details of automatic rules, and manage custom rules.

tags.name: api/security_reports
tags.description: Return data needed by the security reports

tags.name: api/sources
tags.description: Get details on source files. See also api/tests.

tags.name: api/system
tags.description: Get system details, and perform some management actions, such as restarting, and initiating a database migration (as part of a system upgrade).

tags.name: api/user_tokens
tags.description: List, create, and delete a user's access tokens.

tags.name: api/webhooks
tags.description: Webhooks allow to notify external services when a project analysis is done

tags.name: api/webservices
tags.description: Get information on the web api supported on this instance.

tags.name: batch
tags.description: Get JAR files and referentials for batch

post.summary: Create a SonarQube project with the information from the provided Azure DevOps project.
post.description: Create a SonarQube project with the information from the provided Azure DevOps project. Autoconfigure pull request decoration mechanism. Requires the 'Create Projects' permission

parameters.name: almSetting
parameters.description: DevOps Platform configuration key. This parameter is optional if you have only one Azure integration.

parameters.name: newCodeDefinitionType
parameters.description: Project New Code Definition Type New code definitions of the following types are allowed: - PREVIOUS_VERSION - NUMBER_OF_DAYS - REFERENCE_BRANCH - will default to the main branch.

parameters.name: newCodeDefinitionValue
parameters.description: Project New Code Definition Value For each new code definition type, a different value is expected: - no value, when the new code definition type is PREVIOUS_VERSION and REFERENCE_BRANCH - a number between 1 and 90, when the new code definition type is NUMBER_OF_DAYS

parameters.name: projectName
parameters.description: Azure project name

parameters.name: repositoryName
parameters.description: Azure repository name

parameters.name: auth
parameters.description: Whether to also set up GitHub authentication (sign-in) using this App.

parameters.description: Whether to create the DevOps Platform integration (project import / PR analysis) for this App.

parameters.description: Unique key of the GitHub instance setting that will be created. Required when 'devops' is true.

parameters.name: filter
parameters.description: Filter of projects on name, key, measure value, quality gate, language, tag or whether a project is a favorite or not. The filter must be encoded to form a valid URL (for example '=' must be replaced by '%3D'). Examples of use: - to filter my favorite projects with a failed quality gate and a coverage greater than or equals to 60% and a coverage strictly lower than 80%: filter="alert_status = ERROR and isFavorite and coverage >= 60 and coverage - to filter projects with a reliability, security and maintainability rating equals or worse than B: filter="reliability_rating>=2 and security_rating>=2 and sqale_rating>=2" - to filter projects without duplication data: filter="duplicated_lines_density = NO_DATA"To filter on project name or key, use the 'query' keyword, for instance : filter='query = "Sonar"'. To filter on a numeric metric, provide the metric key. These are the supported metric keys: - alert_status - coverage - duplicated_lines_density - lines - ncloc - ncloc_language_distribution - new_coverage - new_duplicated_lines_density - new_lines - new_maintainability_rating - new_reliability_rating - new_sca_rating_any_issue - new_security_hotspots_reviewed - new_security_rating - new_security_review_rating - new_software_quality_maintainability_rating - new_software_quality_reliability_rating - new_software_quality_security_rating - reliability_rating - sca_rating_any_issue - security_hotspots_reviewed - security_rating - security_review_rating - software_quality_maintainability_rating - software_quality_reliability_rating - software_quality_security_rating - sqale_rating To filter on a rating, provide the corresponding metric key (ex: reliability_rating for reliability rating). The possible values are: - '1' for rating A - '2' for rating B - '3' for rating C - '4' for rating D - '5' for rating ETo filter on a Quality Gate status use the metric key 'alert_status'. Only the '=' operator can be used. The possible values are: - 'OK' for Passed - 'WARN' for Warning - 'ERROR' for FailedTo filter on languages use the 'languages' keyword: - to filter on a single language you can use 'languages = java' - to filter on several languages you must use 'languages IN (java, js)'Use the WS api/languages/list to find the key of a language. To filter on tags use the 'tags' keyword: - to filter on one tag you can use tags = finance - to filter on several tags you must use tags in (offshore, java)To filter on a qualifier use key 'qualifier'. Only the '=' operator can be used. The possible values are: - TRK - for projects - APP - for applications

parameters.name: p
parameters.description: 1-based page number

parameters.name: ps
parameters.description: Page size. Must be greater than 0 and less or equal than 500

parameters.name: s
parameters.description: Sort projects by numeric metric key, quality gate status (using 'alert_status'), last analysis date (using 'analysisDate'), project name or creationDate (using 'filter').

get.summary: Get the list of all the plugins available for installation on the SonarQube instance, sorted by plugin name.
get.description: Get the list of all the plugins available for installation on the SonarQube instance, sorted by plugin name. Plugin information is retrieved from Update Center. Date and time at which Update Center was last refreshed is provided in the response. Update status values are: - COMPATIBLE: plugin is compatible with current SonarQube instance. - INCOMPATIBLE: plugin is not compatible with current SonarQube instance. - REQUIRES_SYSTEM_UPGRADE: plugin requires SonarQube to be upgraded before being installed. - DEPS_REQUIRE_SYSTEM_UPGRADE: at least one plugin on which the plugin is dependent requires SonarQube to be upgraded.Require 'Administer System' permission.

parameters.description:
Filter the users based on the last connection date field.

parameters.name: q

parameters.name: slLastConnectedAfter
parameters.description:
Filter the users based on the sonar lint last connection date field
Only users who interacted with this instance using SonarLint at or after the date will be returned.
The format must be ISO 8601 datetime format (YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ss±hhmm)

parameters.name: slLastConnectedBefore
parameters.description:
Filter the users based on the sonar lint last connection date field.
